frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Aurat ko izzat deni chahiye, beta, kyunki wahi kisi ki maa hoti hai, kisi ki beti, kisi ghar ki bahu. Jo aurat ghar ko sambhalti hai aur bachchon ke liye apni poori zindagi laga deti hai, usse badhkar samman kis ka? Aurat ki asli pehchan uske rishte hi hote hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames a woman's worth and dignity primarily through her roles as mother, daughter, and daughter-in-law rather than as an individual person. It encodes relational framing and sacrifice glorification, making the statement sound respectful on the surface while erasing women's independent identity.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 16 is exceptionally good at mathematics. Her school has recommended she be enrolled in a special coaching programme in another part of the city that would require her to travel by bus alone. Your son and daughter in law are considering it. You are sitting with your son in the evening after the daughter in law has raised the topic. You are telling your son what you think and why you are hesitant and what can happen to a girl who starts travelling alone at 16 and whether the mathematics is worth it.
